perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof, the Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to those of its employees and officers who need to know the same for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to those of its employees and officers who need to know the same for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Subject to the provisions of Clauses 4 and 7 hereof', the obligations become absolute. However, Clauses 4 and 7 contain exceptions regarding Freedom of Information requests and the agreement's term. This creates a contradiction, as the Receiving Party is now obligated to keep all Proprietary Information confidential without exception, despite Clauses 4 and 7 outlining situations where disclosure is permissible or the agreement has expired, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Dstl nor The University shall be in breach of this Agreement where it can show that any disclosure of information is made solely and to the extent necessary to comply with the Freedom of Information Act 2000 or the Environmental Information Regulations 2004. Where Dstl or The University is required to make a disclosure of Proprietary Information of a Disclosing Party under the Act or the Regulations it shall, to the extent permitted by the time for c ompliance under the Act or the Regulations, give the Disclosing Party the opportunity to make representations concerning disclosure. The Disclosing Party, however, acknowledges and accepts that its representations may not be determinative and that the dec ision whether to disclose the Proprietary Information at issue, in order to comply with the Act or the Regulations, is a matter in which Dstl or The University shall exercise its own discretion, subject always to the provisions of the Act or the Regulation s and based upon the principle to limit said disclosure to only the requested and required information under the Act or the Regulations. Dstl or The University shall in any event provide prior notification to the Disclosing Party of any decision to disclo se any of its Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause making it subject to Clauses 4 and 7, which contained exceptions for Freedom of Information requests. The changed text removes this clause, creating an absolute obligation to keep information confidential. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that disclosure is permissible under certain conditions like Freedom of Information Act requests. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder, and any copies thereof made by a Receiving Party, shall be and remain the Disclosing Party's property and shall be delivered up promptly by the Receiving Party to the Disclosing Party on receipt of the Disclosing Party's written request therefor.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder, and any copies thereof made by a Receiving Party, shall be and remain the Disclosing Party's property.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'and shall be delivered up promptly by the Receiving Party to the Disclosing Party on receipt of the Disclosing Party's written request therefor,' the contract now lacks a clear mechanism for the return of Proprietary Information. This creates an internal contradiction if the agreement is terminated or if the Disclosing Party requires the information back. Section 7 states that 'The obligations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years,' but without the delivery clause, the Disclosing Party has no explicit way to reclaim their information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remai n in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties. The obli gations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years.
perturbation.justification: The omission in the 'Changed Text' of the requirement to return Proprietary Information upon request directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text'. The contradicted text states that obligations related to Proprietary Information survive termination, implying the Disclosing Party retains rights. However, the removal of the delivery clause in the 'Changed Text' removes the Disclosing Party's explicit mechanism to reclaim their information upon termination (or at any time), creating a clear and direct conflict and preventing the enforcement of the obligations that should have survived termination. The original text provided a clear mechanism for the return of proprietary information, while the 'Changed Text' removes that, which causes a contradiction based on the explanation that the Disclosing party has no explicit way to reclaim their information, which should have survived after termination.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remai n in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties. The obli gations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in force for a period of two (2) years following the date first above written after which it will terminate unless renewed by mutual consent in writing or to the extent that it is superseded by another agreement or contract between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The obligations and restrictions relating to the disclosure and use of Proprietary Information shall however survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of seven (7) years,' the contract now implies that all obligations cease upon termination after two years. This directly contradicts section 2 which places restrictions and obligations on the receiving party to protect the Proprietary Information. If section 7 states these restrictions cease after two years, it makes section 2 ineffective after the two-year mark and creates in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party undertakes: (a) to keep confidential and not disclose to any third party, except with the written permission of the Disclosing Party, any part, or the whole, of any Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreem ent; (b) not to copy Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement except as is reasonably necessary for the Purpose; (c) not to use Proprietary Information disclosed to it under this Agreement other than for the Purpose, except with th e prior written permission of the Disclosing Party; (d) to restrict access to the Proprietary Information disclosed to it under the terms of this Agreement to those of its employees and officers who need to know the same for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly stated that obligations relating to proprietary information survive termination for seven years. The changed text omits this sentence, implying that all obligations cease after the initial two-year term. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which places ongoing confidentiality and usage restrictions on the receiving party regarding proprietary information. The omission creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted section becomes unenforceable or ineffective after two years, based on the changed text, because the restrictions are intended to last longer than the initial two-year term. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parti es concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose. The Agreement shall not be amended except by written agreement signed by authorised representatives of all Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parti es concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The Agreement shall not be amended except by written agreement signed by authorised representatives of all Parties.', it introduces ambiguity on whether modifications to the contract are allowable, even though the contract explicitly states that the entire existing agreement has been made. In section 8, it says it is the entirety of the existing agreement, and by removing the phrase it creates ambiguity whether there are or aren't further considerations that need to be made, contradictory to what the original section implies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parti es concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement cannot be amended except in writing signed by all parties. By removing this clause in the changed text, it introduces ambiguity as to whether the agreement can be amended or not. This ambiguity directly contradicts the contradicted text which states 'This Agreement constitutes the entire existing Agreement between the Parties concerning the exchange of Proprietary Information for the Purpose.' because if the agreement can be amended without written consent then it isn't the 'entire existing agreement'. According to the Perturbation Explanations, 'Omissions occur when essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.' and this falls under that.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall attempt to solve any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement by means of alternative dispute resolution such as b ut without limitation mediati on or mini -trial. Upon the occurrence of the dispute, the Parties shall define the type and the rules for the implementation of such alternative resolution. The Parties expressly agree that after a period of two (2) months after the occurrence of the dis pute and provided that the dispute remains unsolved, it shall be finally settled under the rules of Arbitration of the London Court of International Arbitration by one or more arbitrators appointed in accordance with the said rules. The place of arbitratio n shall be London (UK). The language of arbitration shall be the English Language. The Parties hereby agree to exclude the right of appeal to the High Court under section 69 of the Arbitration Act 1996 and the right for an application to be made to the Hi gh Court under the corresponding section of the Act. Each Party recognises and acknowledges that in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement it shall have the right to apply for injunctive relief or other appropriate orders before an y competent court to restrain the other as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall attempt to solve any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement by means of alternative dispute resolution such as b ut without limitation mediati on or mini -trial. Upon the occurrence of the dispute, the Parties shall define the type and the rules for the implementation of such alternative resolution. Each Party recognises and acknowledges that in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement it shall have the right to apply for injunctive relief or other appropriate orders before an y competent court to restrain the other as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section on arbitration, this introduces the in-text contradiction that the Parties agree to solve the agreement disputes by means of alternative dispute resolution, but there is no agreement on the arbitration rules to do so. This implies that alternative dispute resolution will be sought but does not define the course of actions to make it happen which makes it contradictory to the statement 'the parties shall attempt to solve'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall attempt to solve any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement by means of alternative dispute resolution such as b ut without limitation mediati on or mini -trial. Upon the occurrence of the dispute, the Parties shall define the type and the rules for the implementation of such alternative resolution. Each Party recognises and acknowledges that in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement it shall have the right to apply for injunctive relief or other appropriate orders before an y competent court to restrain the other as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a detailed arbitration clause specifying the rules, location, language, and exclusion of appeal rights. The changed text removes this arbitration clause. The contradicted text refers to the parties attempting to solve any dispute arising out of or in connection with the Agreement by means of alternative dispute resolution. By removing the agreed-upon arbitration process, the changed text creates a contradiction. While parties agree to attempt to solve any dispute by means of alternative dispute resolution, there is no longer a pre-agreed mechanism for arbitration, making the attempt to solve the dispute potentially impossible and thus contradicting the original intention and creating uncertainty regarding dispute resolution.
